canonical_question: How do I design an IoT data ingestion pipeline?
aliases: How to build an IoT streaming pipeline
constraints: Always implement backpressure handling between ingestion and processing layers to prevent data loss during traffic spikes
Use TLS 1.2+ for all device-to-broker connections; never transmit telemetry over unencrypted channels
Design for at-least-once delivery by default; exactly-once requires idempotent consumers or deduplication at the storage layer
Time-series databases require monotonically increasing timestamps; clock drift on edge devices must be corrected before ingestion

skip_this_unit_if.condition: You need to process batch ETL data from databases or files, not streaming device telemetry
You need real-time analytics on user clickstream data, not IoT sensor data

often_confused_with.label: Message Queue Architecture (task queues vs. event streams)

inputs_needed.question: What protocol do your IoT devices use to send data?
How many devices will send data concurrently?
What is your acceptable end-to-end latency from device to queryable storage?
